rootCauseClusters.name: Auth Death Spiral
rootCauseClusters.description: No auth state machine. Dead auth (expired refresh token) is treated as transient failure. PollingTimer retries forever with a known-bad token, producing 401s every 5 minutes indefinitely. No user notification, no circuit breaker, no recovery path. This is the root cause of the 7x recurrence.

rootCauseClusters.name: Cache Freshness Lies
rootCauseClusters.description: Multiple paths cause stale/missing cache data to report itself as fresh: missing fetchedAt defaults to new Date(), getStaleness(null) returns 'normal', backward clock jump produces negative age that reads as 'fresh'. User sees confident-looking data that may be hours old.

rootCauseClusters.name: User Blindness
rootCauseClusters.description: User has no way to understand or fix the problem: forceRefresh silently dropped, staleness display has no action hint, 'normal' state is ambiguous (could mean 'recent data' or 'never fetched').

findings.description: refreshOAuthToken returns null for ALL failure modes (400 bad grant, 401 revoked, network error, timeout). Caller cannot distinguish terminal (dead refresh token) from transient (network blip) failures.
findings.suggestion: Return a discriminated union: {ok: token} | {error: 'refresh_token_expired'} | {error: 'network'} | {error: 'server_error'}. Map HTTP 400/401 to terminal, network/timeout to transient.

findings.description: After refresh fails, code falls through to try the known-expired token. This always produces a 401 from the API, which the extension treats as a transient failure. Entry point to the death spiral.
findings.suggestion: When refresh returns a terminal error, return null immediately without trying the expired token. Or propagate the error type so PollingTimer can make an informed decision.

findings.description: 401 handler logs 'Will retry when Claude Code refreshes it' -- but nothing detects when Claude Code refreshes. The CredentialsWatcher only watches for tier changes, not token changes. This message implies automatic recovery that will never happen.
findings.suggestion: Either make CredentialsWatcher signal the PollingTimer on token changes, or change the log message to reflect reality: 'Auth expired. User must re-authenticate in Claude Code.'

findings.description: consecutiveFailures increments without bound. No circuit breaker. After N failures (e.g. 5), should transition to 'dead' state, stop polling, and notify user.
findings.suggestion: Add AUTH_DEAD_THRESHOLD = 5. When consecutiveFailures >= threshold and last error was auth-related, stop polling and emit an 'authDead' event. Resume when credentials file changes.

findings.description: fetchFn signature is () => Promise<ApiUsageData | null>. The null erases all error information. Timer cannot distinguish auth failure, network error, rate limiting, or server error.
findings.suggestion: Change to () => Promise<FetchResult> where FetchResult = {ok: ApiUsageData} | {error: 'auth_dead' | 'network' | 'rate_limited' | 'server_error'}.

findings.description: Invalid/missing fetchedAt defaults to new Date(), making corrupted or old cache data appear freshly fetched. getStaleness returns 'fresh' for data that could be hours old.
findings.suggestion: Return null from readCache when fetchedAt is missing or invalid, rather than defaulting to now. Let the caller decide how to handle missing data.

findings.description: getStaleness(null) returns 'normal'. This means 'API never connected' displays identically to '30-60 minute old data'. User cannot distinguish the two states.
findings.suggestion: Add an 'unavailable' staleness level for null fetchedAt. Status bar should show a distinct indicator when API has never been reached.

findings.description: forceRefresh() is a silent no-op when isTickInFlight is true. User's explicit refresh action is dropped with no feedback. Status bar shows 'Refreshing...' spinner but nothing actually happens.
findings.suggestion: Queue the force refresh request. When the in-flight tick completes, immediately run another tick instead of scheduling at the normal interval.

findings.description: refreshOAuthToken does read-modify-write on .credentials.json without file locking or atomic write (temp+rename). Claude Code writes the same file. Concurrent writes can corrupt tokens.
findings.suggestion: Use temp+rename pattern (like usageCache.ts does). Or better: don't write to the credentials file at all -- let Claude Code own it. Just read and use tokens, don't refresh them.

findings.description: Backward clock jump produces negative ageMs. All threshold checks (ageMs < 30min, etc.) pass because negative < positive. getStaleness returns 'fresh' for potentially hours-old data.
findings.suggestion: Add guard: if (ageMs < 0) return 'stale'; at the top of getStaleness.

findings.description: Hardcoded OAuth client_id. If Anthropic rotates or revokes this ID, the extension silently fails with no recovery path short of an extension update.
findings.suggestion: Make configurable via extension settings with the current value as default.

findings.description: Staleness tooltip shows 'API data is Xm old' but provides no action hint. User doesn't know whether to click refresh, restart VS Code, or re-authenticate in Claude Code.
findings.suggestion: Add action hint based on auth state: 'Click to refresh' for transient issues, 'Re-authenticate in Claude Code' for auth death.

planAttacks.attack: No auth state machine -- system cannot distinguish terminal from transient failures
planAttacks.defense: Extension is passive observer, Claude Code owns auth

planAttacks.attack: null does too much work as error signal -- erases all diagnostic information
planAttacks.defense: Simplicity, timer doesn't need to know why

planAttacks.attack: Cache fetchedAt fallback to new Date() is a time bomb
planAttacks.defense: Better to show stale data than no data

planAttacks.attack: Staleness thresholds too generous for 2-min polling interval
